title: Expand type alias parser test coverage: error cases, edge cases, precedence
analysis: All test coverage requested by this task ALREADY EXISTS in type_alias_tests.rs. The file contains 20 passing tests covering every category listed in the task description: (1) Error cases — parse_type_alias_missing_name_no_panic, parse_type_alias_missing_equals_no_panic, parse_type_alias_empty_rhs_no_panic, plus 3 malformed dimensional type tests (missing right/left/both operands); (2) Type params + dimensional RHS — parse_type_alias_type_params_with_dimensional_rhs tests `type Velocity<T> = T / Time`; (3) Nested parameterized types — parse_type_alias_nested_parameterized_types tests `type Registry = Map<String, List<Int>>`; (4) Mixed operator precedence — parse_type_alias_mixed_operator_precedence tests `type X = Mass / Time * Scalar` and asserts outer node is `*` with inner `/`. These were likely added alongside or shortly after the dependency task 144. All 20 tests compile and pass. No new code is needed — the task is effectively pre-completed.

steps.description: Verify all 20 existing type_alias_tests pass: `cargo test --test type_alias_tests`. Confirm error-case tests (missing_name, missing_equals, empty_rhs, missing_right_operand, missing_left_operand, missing_both_operands) assert !errors.is_empty(). Confirm type_params_with_dimensional_rhs, nested_parameterized_types, and mixed_operator_precedence tests exist and pass.

design_decisions.decision: No new tests needed — all requested coverage already present
design_decisions.rationale: The file already contains all 4 categories of tests listed in the task: error cases (3 malformed input + 3 malformed dimensional), type params with dimensional RHS, nested parameterized types, and mixed operator precedence. All 20 tests pass. Adding redundant tests would create maintenance burden without coverage gain.

reuse.how: Shared helper that wraps reify_syntax::parse() and returns (Vec<Declaration>, Vec<ParseError>). Already used by all 20 tests.
Consistent pattern: parse → assert errors empty/non-empty → match Declaration::TypeAlias(ta) → assert fields. Used across all test files in the crate.
